expectations.text: Extracts 1-2 focused findings — does not inflate to 5+ findings from a single observation
expectations.evidence: 5 distinct findings are extracted: Finding 1 (wrong circle type — competitor circles), Finding 2 (metrics circles have no visible label), Finding 3 (competitors show as large empty circles at low zoom), Finding 4 (no zoom-aware suppression for competitors at country scope), Finding 5 (marker image generation uses black fill, not green). This inflates one user observation into 5 code-archaeology findings, most of which go well beyond the user's stated concern.

expectations.text: SBE proposes concrete visual behavior — zoom threshold, store count display, or specific sizing — not vague 'should look better'
expectations.evidence: AC-1 specifies: 'COMPETITORS_CIRCLES layer must not be rendered' at GeographicScope.COUNTRY. AC-4 specifies: 'each circle shows its formattedCount label (e.g. "1.2K", "450")'. AC-5 enumerates exactly what should be visible at country zoom: colored state fills, small red location dots, black metrics circles with count labels. The implementation note provides a concrete code snippet with a scope guard. All are concrete, not vague.

expectations.text: Captures the first-impression business rationale — user said 'first thing users see when they open the map'
expectations.evidence: The spec header states: 'Priority: High — this is the first-impression view (default zoom on map open)'. AC-5 is titled 'No empty circles appear at country zoom as first impression' and its Given clause starts with 'the user opens the Measurement Map for the first time (default zoom ~3.75)'. The first-impression business rationale is present and prominent.

expectations.text: Does NOT scope into other map issues (flow lines, choropleth colors, panel layout)
expectations.evidence: The findings analysis identifies the green circles as competitor circles rather than the circles the user was actually describing, which drives the entire spec toward suppressing COMPETITORS_CIRCLES. This reinterpretation is based on code analysis ('competitorsCircles.strokeColor = "#0eac1c" — matches the green in the screenshot exactly') rather than user narration. The user described circles that 'look broken' — the without_skill output invents a cause (wrong layer type appearing) and specifies a fix (suppress COMPETITORS_CIRCLES) that may not match the actual issue. The findings section also addresses 'competitor data' rendering at all zoom levels — a scope the user never raised.
